description_long: This house features a prominent gable roof with a secondary dormer section, both covered in dark gray metal or composite panels. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal siding, giving it a clean, modern Craftsman appearance. The front facade includes a set of French doors flanked by symmetrical windows, and a row of clerestory windows above, providing ample natural light. A covered porch area is visible on one side, supported by simple posts and open to the front, ideal for outdoor seating. The rear and side elevations are more utilitarian, with minimal window openings and a continuous siding finish. The overall structure is single-story, compact, and well-suited for suburban or urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story house with a dark gable roof, horizontal siding, French doors, clerestory windows, and a covered porch, reflecting a modern Craftsman style.
description_view_0: The front gable end is visible, showing the covered porch area and partial support beams beneath the roof.
description_view_1: The long side of the house is shown, highlighting the extended roofline and horizontal siding with minimal window openings.
description_view_2: The rear gable end is visible, featuring a plain wall with horizontal siding and no windows or doors.
description_view_3: The main facade is shown, with French doors, symmetrical windows, clerestory windows above, and the covered porch to the right.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with clerestory windows above main entry
attribute_entryway_design: French doors with sidelights, clerestory windows above, covered porch supported by posts
